questions.text: Resolution power is the ability to?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Enhance color contrast in an image
options.explanation: Resolution power is not related to color contrast enhancement in an image. Resolution power is specifically concerned with the ability to distinguish fine details or separate two close points or objects in an image, regardless of the color or contrast.

Distinguish between two close points is the correct description of resolution power. It is the capacity of an imaging system to differentiate or resolve fine details that are close together. In other words, it determines the level of detail that can be observed and distinguished in an image.

Distinguish amongst organelles is not an accurate description of resolution power. While resolution power is relevant in microscopy to distinguish fine details, such as organelles within a cell, it is not specific to distinguishing amongst organelles.

Magnify an image is not the same as resolution power. Magnification refers to the increase in apparent size of an object, whereas resolution power relates to the ability to distinguish fine details or resolve separate points or objects.

questions.explanation: Resolution power is the ability to distinguish between two close objects. It is the ability of a system to distinguish between two close points as two separate points. Resolving power of a microscope depends upon the numerical aperature of the objective lens system and its wavelength.

questions.text: The latest model for plasma membrane is called the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The lamellar model is an outdated model that was proposed in the 1930s. It suggested that the plasma membrane consists of multiple layers of lipid molecules arranged in a regular, repeating pattern. However, this model has been replaced by more accurate and dynamic models.

The unit membrane model is also an outdated model that proposes that all cellular membranes have a similar basic structure. It suggested that all membranes are made up of a single layer of lipid molecules sandwiched between two layers of protein molecules. However, this model is oversimplified and not representative of the actual complexity of cellular membranes.

The fluid mosaic model is the most widely accepted and current model for the plasma membrane. In 1972, S.J. Singer and G.L. Nicolson proposed this model. According to this model, the plasma membrane consists of a phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins. The lipid bilayer is fluid, meaning the individual lipid molecules can move laterally within the membrane. The proteins are not static but form a dynamic mosaic pattern floating within the lipid bilayer. The fluidity and mosaic arrangement of lipids and proteins in the membrane allow various functions, such as cell signaling, transport, and maintaining cell structure.

The term "molecular lipid model" is not commonly used to describe the structure of the plasma membrane. It may refer to various aspects of lipid molecules and their interactions in the membrane, but it is not a widely recognized model for the entire plasma membrane structure.

questions.explanation: The fluid mosaic model is the most widely accepted and current model for the plasma membrane. In 1972, S.J. Singer and G.L. Nicolson proposed this model. According to this model, the plasma membrane consists of a phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins. The lipid bilayer is fluid, meaning the individual lipid molecules can move laterally within the membrane. The proteins are not static but form a dynamic mosaic pattern floating within the lipid bilayer. The fluidity and mosaic arrangement of lipids and proteins in the membrane allow various functions, such as cell signaling, transport, and maintaining cell structure.

questions.text: Magnification of a compound microscope is not connected to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Numerical aperture (NA) is a measure of the ability of the lens in the microscope to gather light and resolve fine details of the specimen. It is related to the angle of light acceptance and the refractive index of the medium between the specimen and the lens. Higher numerical aperture generally results in better resolution and higher quality images, but it does not directly determine the magnification of the microscope.

The focal length of the objective lens in a compound microscope determines the magnification and working distance of the microscope. Magnification is directly related to the focal length of the objective lens. A shorter focal length results in higher magnification and also reduces the working distance (the distance between the objective lens and the specimen). Longer focal length objectives offer lower magnification but provide more working distance.

The focal length of the eyepiece (ocular lens) also plays a crucial role in determining the overall magnification of the compound microscope. The eyepiece further magnifies the image produced by the objective lens. An eyepiece of shorter focal length provides higher magnification, whereas an eyepiece of longer focal length provides lower magnification.

Tube length refers to the distance between the objective lens and the eyepiece in a compound microscope. In most modern microscopes, the standard tube length is 160 mm. The tube length is not directly related to the magnification of the microscope. Instead, it ensures that the optical components (objective and eyepiece) are appropriately aligned to produce a clear and focused image for the observer. So, indirectly, it determines the magnification of the microscope by appropriately adjusting the optical components.

questions.explanation: Numerical aperture (NA) is a measure of the ability of the lens in the microscope to gather light and resolve fine details of the specimen. It is related to the angle of light acceptance and the refractive index of the medium between the specimen and the lens. Higher numerical aperture generally results in better resolution and higher quality images, but it does not determine the magnification of the microscope. Other terms given in the options either directly or indirectly determine the magnification of the compound microscope.

questions.text: A bivalent consists of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. A bivalent, also known as a tetrad, is formed during meiosis when two homologous chromosomes come together and align closely. Each homologous chromosome consists of two sister chromatids, and they are connected by a centromere. So, each bivalent contains four chromatids and two centromeres (one centromere for each homologous chromosome).

This option is incorrect. As mentioned earlier, each homologous chromosome in a bivalent has two sister chromatids connected by a centromere. So, each bivalent contains four chromatids and two centromeres (one centromere for each homologous chromosome).

This option is correct. A bivalent, also known as a tetrad, consists of two homologous chromosomes, with each homologous chromosome having two sister chromatids connected by a centromere. So, each bivalent contains four chromatids and two centromeres (one centromere for each homologous chromosome).

This option is incorrect. A bivalent, as stated above, consists of two homologous chromosomes, and each homologous chromosome has two sister chromatids connected by a centromere. So, each bivalent contains four chromatids and two centromeres (not four centromeres).

questions.explanation: A bivalent, also known as a tetrad, consists of two homologous chromosomes, with each homologous chromosome having two sister chromatids connected by a centromere. So, each bivalent contains four chromatids and two centromeres (one centromere for each homologous chromosome).

questions.text: Nucleoproteins are synthesised in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Nucleoplasm refers to the fluid inside the cell nucleus. While many cellular processes related to gene expression and RNA processing occur in the nucleoplasm, nucleoproteins, which are complexes of proteins and nucleic acids (such as DNA and RNA), are not synthesized here.

The nuclear envelope is a double membrane that surrounds the cell nucleus. It contains nuclear pores that allow the exchange of molecules between the nucleus and the cytoplasm. While some nucleoproteins may be involved in nuclear envelope structure and function, the synthesis of nucleoproteins does not primarily occur in the nuclear envelope.

The nucleolus is a distinct sub-structure within the cell nucleus and is primarily responsible for the assembly of ribosomes. While the nucleolus is rich in ribosomal RNA (rRNA) and proteins, the synthesis of nucleoproteins, as the term is commonly understood, does not primarily occur in the nucleolus.

This option is correct. Nucleoproteins, which are complexes of proteins and nucleic acids, including histones, transcription factors, and RNA-binding proteins, are primarily synthesized in the cytoplasm. These proteins play essential roles in gene regulation, DNA replication, RNA processing, and other cellular processes involving nucleic acids.

questions.explanation: The cytoplasm is the region where metabolic processes like PROTEIN SYNTHESIS occur. Nucleoproteins, which are complexes of proteins and nucleic acids, including histones, transcription factors, and RNA-binding proteins, are primarily synthesized in the cytoplasm. These proteins play essential roles in gene regulation, DNA replication, RNA processing, and other cellular processes involving nucleic acids.

questions.text: Polyribosomes are aggregates of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Ribosomes are molecular machines responsible for protein synthesis, and rRNA (ribosomal RNA) is a key component of ribosomes. However, polyribosomes are not aggregates of ribosomes and rRNA. Instead, rRNA is a major structural and functional component of ribosomes.

This option is incorrect. While rRNA is an essential component of ribosomes, polyribosomes are not composed solely of rRNA. Polyribosomes involve ribosomes and mRNA (messenger RNA), as explained in option D.

This option is incorrect. Peroxisomes are organelles in the cell responsible for various metabolic processes, including detoxification and the breakdown of fatty acids. However, polyribosomes are not aggregates of peroxisomes.

options.text: Several ribosomes held together by string of mRNA
options.explanation: This option is correct. Polyribosomes, also known as polysomes, are clusters of several ribosomes bound to a single mRNA molecule. These ribosomes work simultaneously to translate the mRNA sequence into a polypeptide chain during protein synthesis. Polyribosomes play a critical role in increasing the efficiency of protein production in the cell.

questions.explanation: Polyribosomes, also known as polysomes, are clusters of several ribosomes bound to a single mRNA molecule. These ribosomes work simultaneously to translate the mRNA sequence into a polypeptide chain during protein synthesis. Polyribosomes play a critical role in increasing the efficiency of protein production in the cell.

questions.text: Plasma membrane is made of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is not completely correct. The plasma membrane, also known as the cell membrane, is indeed composed of proteins and carbohydrates in addition to lipids. The carbohydrates are often present as part of glycoproteins or glycolipids on the outer surface of the membrane and play roles in cell recognition and signaling.

This option is partially correct. The plasma membrane is primarily composed of proteins and lipids. The lipids in the membrane form a phospholipid bilayer, which provides the basic structure of the membrane. Proteins are embedded in the lipid bilayer and have various functions, such as transport, cell signaling, and structural support. However, traces of carbohydrates are also present in addition to proteins and lipids.

This option is correct. The plasma membrane is a dynamic and selectively permeable barrier that surrounds the cell, and it is made up of three main types of biomolecules: proteins, lipids, and carbohydrates. The lipids, mainly phospholipids, form a bilayer, while the proteins are embedded in the lipid bilayer or extend through it. The carbohydrates are often found on the outer surface of the membrane as part of glycoproteins or glycolipids.

options.text: Proteins, some nucleic acids and lipids
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. While proteins and lipids are indeed key components of the plasma membrane, nucleic acids, such as DNA and RNA, are not part of the membrane's structure. Nucleic acids are primarily located in the cell nucleus or other specific cellular compartments, and they are not a fundamental part of the plasma membrane.

questions.explanation: The plasma membrane is a dynamic and selectively permeable barrier that surrounds the cell, and it is made up of three main types of biomolecules: proteins, lipids, and carbohydrates. The lipids, mainly phospholipids, form a bilayer, while the proteins are embedded in the lipid bilayer or extend through it. The carbohydrates are often found on the outer surface of the membrane as part of glycoproteins or glycolipids.

questions.text: Organelles can be separated from cell homogenate through:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Chromatography is a technique used to separate and analyze the components of a mixture based on their interactions with a stationary phase and a mobile phase. While chromatography is a powerful tool for separating molecules, it is not commonly used to separate organelles from a cell homogenate.

X-ray diffraction is a technique used to study the crystal structure of materials, including biological molecules such as DNA and proteins. It is not typically used for separating organelles from a cell homogenate.

This option is correct. Differential centrifugation is a widely used method for separating organelles from a cell homogenate. It takes advantage of differences in size, shape, and density of cellular components. During the process, the cell homogenate is subjected to a series of centrifugation steps at different speeds, allowing the larger and denser organelles to pellet at lower speeds, while smaller components and debris remain in the supernatant.

Auto-radiography is a technique used to detect and visualize radioactive substances in a sample. It involves exposing the sample to a photographic film or emulsion that records the radiation emitted by the radioactive substance. While this technique can be used to study cellular processes involving radioactive compounds, it is not used for separating organelles from a cell homogenate.

questions.explanation: Differential centrifugation is a widely used method for separating organelles from a cell homogenate. It takes advantage of the differences in size, shape, and density of the cellular components. During the process, the cell homogenate is subjected to a series of centrifugation steps at different speeds, allowing the larger and denser organelles to pellet at lower speeds while smaller components and debris remain in the supernatant.

questions.text: Acetabularia used in Hammerling’s nucleocytoplasmic experiments is a/an:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Fungi are a separate kingdom in the domain Eukarya and are known for being multicellular organisms. They consist of hyphae, which form a mycelium and reproduce through spores. Acetabularia is not a fungus, and it is not unicellular.
As mentioned earlier, fungi are typically multicellular organisms. Acetabularia is not a fungus, so this option is incorrect.

This option is correct. Acetabularia is a unicellular green alga belonging to the family Polyphysaceae. It is well-known for its large size and distinct morphological features. Acetabularia has a single cell that is uninucleate, meaning it contains a single nucleus. It is an excellent model organism for nucleocytoplasmic studies and was extensively used in Hammerling's experiments to investigate the role of the nucleus in determining cell development and characteristics.
Acetabularia is unicellular, but it has a single nucleus. It is not multinucleate, so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Acetabularia is a unicellular green alga belonging to the family Polyphysaceae. It is well-known for its large size and distinct morphological features. Acetabularia has a single cell that is uninucleate, meaning it contains a single nucleus. It is an excellent model organism for nucleocytoplasmic studies and was extensively used in Hammerling's experiments to investigate the role of the nucleus in determining cell development and characteristics.

questions.text: According to fluid mosaic model, plasma membrane is composed of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While phospholipids are indeed a major component of the plasma membrane according to the fluid mosaic model, oligosaccharides (short carbohydrate chains) are typically found in glycoproteins and glycolipids on the outer surface of the membrane. Carbohydrates are not part of the main structure of the plasma membrane. So, this option is incorrect.

Similar to option A, phospholipids are a crucial component of the plasma membrane. However, hemicellulose is a type of polysaccharide commonly found in the cell walls of plants, not the plasma membrane of cells. So, this option is incorrect.

This option is partially correct. The fluid mosaic model indeed states that the plasma membrane is primarily composed of phospholipids forming a phospholipid bilayer. Integral proteins are another key component of the plasma membrane. They are embedded within the lipid bilayer in a mosaic manner and span through the membrane.

options.text: Phospholipids, extrinsic proteins and intrinsic proteins.
options.explanation: This option is correct. According to the fluid mosaic model, the plasma membrane is a dynamic structure composed of phospholipids forming a lipid bilayer. It also contains integral proteins that are embedded within the lipid bilayer and extrinsic proteins that are loosely attached to the membrane's surface. Together, the phospholipids and proteins form the fluid mosaic pattern, where the proteins float in the lipid bilayer like pieces of a mosaic.

questions.explanation: According to the fluid mosaic model, the plasma membrane is a dynamic structure composed of phospholipids forming a lipid bilayer. It also contains integral proteins that are embedded within the lipid bilayer and extrinsic proteins that are loosely attached to the membrane's surface. Together, the phospholipids and proteins form the fluid mosaic pattern, where the proteins float in the lipid bilayer like pieces of a mosaic. Hence, option D is the most appropriate answer.

questions.text: Which of the following types of microscope is used to study the internal structures of a cell?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A simple microscope is a type of microscope that consists of a single lens and is used to magnify small objects. It is typically used for low magnification tasks and is not suitable for studying the internal structures of cells. So, this option is incorrect.

A compound microscope is a type of light microscope that uses multiple lenses to magnify the image of a specimen. While it is commonly used in biology labs, it is limited in its ability to resolve structures smaller than the wavelength of visible light. It can be used to study some internal structures of cells, but it has its limitations. So, this is not the best option.

A light microscope, also known as an optical microscope or a compound light microscope, is a type of microscope that uses visible light and a series of lenses to magnify and observe small objects or specimens. It is widely used in biology and is excellent for studying cells and tissues but has limited resolution for observing very fine internal structures of cells. So, this option is also not the best choice.

This option is correct. An electron microscope uses a beam of electrons instead of visible light to image the specimen, providing much higher magnification and resolution than light microscopes. It is capable of resolving the ultrastructure and internal organelles of cells with precise details. Electron microscopes come in two main types: transmission electron microscope (TEM) and scanning electron microscope (SEM).

questions.explanation: An electron microscope uses a beam of electrons instead of visible light to image the specimen, providing much higher magnification and resolution than light microscopes. It is capable of resolving the ultrastructure and internal organelles of cells with precise details. Electron microscopes come in two main types: transmission electron microscope (TEM) and scanning electron microscope (SEM). Hence, option D is the best choice.

questions.text: What is an example of a semi-autonomous organelle found in both animal and plant cells?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is a complex network of membranes involved in the synthesis, folding, and transport of proteins and lipids. It is present in both animal and plant cells, but it is not considered a semi-autonomous organelle. So, this option is incorrect.

Chloroplasts are semi-autonomous organelles found only in plant cells and some protists. They are responsible for photosynthesis, the process by which plants convert sunlight into energy-rich molecules (glucose) using chlorophyll. Chloroplasts contain their own DNA and ribosomes, allowing them to synthesize some of their proteins independently, which makes them semi-autonomous. So, this option is also incorrect.

The Golgi apparatus is an organelle involved in processing, modifying, and sorting proteins and lipids for transport to various destinations within or outside the cell. It is found in both animal and plant cells, but it is not considered a semi-autonomous organelle. So, this option is also incorrect.

This option is correct. Mitochondria are semi-autonomous organelles found in both animal and plant cells. They are often referred to as the "powerhouses" of the cell because they are responsible for generating most of the cell's supply of adenosine triphosphate (ATP), which is used as a source of chemical energy. Mitochondria have their own circular DNA and ribosomes, allowing them to produce some of their proteins independently. This characteristic of having their own genetic material and the ability to self-replicate makes mitochondria semi-autonomous organelles.

questions.explanation: Mitochondria are semi-autonomous organelles found in both animal and plant cells. They are often referred to as the "powerhouses" of the cell because they are responsible for generating most of the cell's supply of adenosine triphosphate (ATP), which is used as a source of chemical energy. Mitochondria have their own circular DNA and ribosomes, allowing them to produce some of their proteins independently. This characteristic of having their own genetic material and the ability to self-replicate makes mitochondria semi-autonomous organelles.

questions.text: Which of the following is not present in plant cells?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct. Flagella are whip-like structures used for cellular movement. While they are present in some types of plant cells (such as the male gametes or sperm cells of certain plants), they are not commonly found in most plant cells. Plant cells do not have flagella as a general feature.
This option is incorrect. Plant cells do have a cell wall. The cell wall is a rigid outer layer made of cellulose that provides structural support and protection to the cell.

This option is incorrect. Plant cells do have a central vacuole, which is a large membrane-bound organelle that stores water, ions, nutrients, and waste products. The central vacuole is a defining feature of plant cells and plays a crucial role in maintaining turgor pressure and regulating cellular functions.

This option is incorrect. Plant cells do have chloroplasts, which are responsible for photosynthesis. Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and other pigments that capture sunlight and convert it into chemical energy in the form of glucose.

questions.explanation: Flagella are whip-like structures used for cellular movement. While they are present in some types of plant cells (such as the male gametes or sperm cells of certain plants), they are not commonly found in most plant cells. Plant cells do not have flagella as a general feature. Hence, option A is the best choice.

questions.text: All of the following are common molecules in membranes of all type of cells except:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Globular proteins, also known as globular or soluble proteins, are three-dimensional, compactly folded proteins that are soluble in water. These proteins perform a wide range of functions within cells, such as enzymatic activities, signal transduction, transport of molecules, and structural support. Globular proteins are present in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cell membranes. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Cholesterol is a type of lipid that is a major component of eukaryotic cell membranes. It helps maintain membrane fluidity and stability by interacting with phospholipids. Cholesterol is present in large amounts (around 20-40 mol%) in eukaryotic plasma membranes, but it is universally absent in the membranes of prokaryotes. Therefore, this option is correct as cholesterol is present in eukaryotic cell membranes but not in the prokaryotic cell membranes.

Carbohydrates are commonly found in cell membranes of both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells in the form of glycoproteins and glycolipids. These carbohydrates play important roles in cell-cell recognition, cell adhesion, and other cellular functions. Hence, this option is also incorrect.

This option is incorrect because phospholipids are a fundamental component of all cell membranes, both prokaryotic and eukaryotic. They are amphipathic molecules, meaning they have both hydrophilic (polar) and hydrophobic (nonpolar) regions. Phospholipids spontaneously arrange themselves in a bilayer structure with their hydrophobic tails facing inward and hydrophilic heads facing outward, creating the basic framework of the cell membrane.

questions.explanation: Cholesterol is a type of lipid that is a major component of eukaryotic cell membranes. It helps maintain membrane fluidity and stability by interacting with phospholipids. Cholesterol is present in large amounts (around 20-40 mol%) in eukaryotic plasma membranes, but it is universally absent in the membranes of prokaryotes. Therefore, the correct option is cholesterol as it is not present is all types of cell membranes.

questions.text: Which of the following describes the fluid mosaic model of plasma membrane structure?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The fluid mosaic model of the plasma membrane proposes a phospholipid bilayer, not a monolayer. In a phospholipid monolayer, there would be only a single layer of phospholipids, which is not the case in the plasma membrane.

This option is correct. The fluid mosaic model describes the structure of the plasma membrane as a phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins. The lipid bilayer consists of two layers of phospholipids, with their hydrophobic tails facing inward and their hydrophilic heads facing outward. The proteins are embedded within the lipid bilayer, making it look like a mosaic.
This option is incorrect. Triglycerides are a type of lipid, but they are not the main components of the plasma membrane. The primary lipid components of the plasma membrane are phospholipids.
This option is incorrect. Similar to option C, triglycerides are not the main components of the plasma membrane, and the plasma membrane is not a monolayer.

questions.explanation: The fluid mosaic model describes the structure of the plasma membrane as a phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins. The lipid bilayer consists of two layers of phospholipids, with their hydrophobic tails facing inward and their hydrophilic heads facing outward. The proteins are interspersed within the lipid bilayer, making it look like a mosaic.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a characteristic feature of the animal cell membrane?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is not the right option. The animal cell membrane provides mechanical support, maintains homeostasis, and regulates the passage of molecules across the membrane. It does not prevent the animal cell from osmotic lysis as that is the function of the cell wall, which is absent in animal cells.

This option is also not the answer. The cell membrane plays a crucial role in maintaining cellular homeostasis by controlling the movement of molecules into and out of the cell. It regulates the transport of nutrients, ions, and waste products, helping to maintain the balance and stability of the cell's internal environment.

This option is also not the answer. The cell membrane is selectively permeable, allowing only specific molecules to enter or exit the cell. It regulates the passage of substances to ensure that essential molecules are taken up and harmful substances are kept out.

This option is the right choice. The cell membrane in animal cells does not prevent the cell from osmotic lysis. Animal cells do not have a cell wall like plant cells, and they are susceptible to osmotic lysis in a hypotonic environment where water can enter the cell freely, causing it to swell and potentially burst.

questions.explanation: The cell membrane in animal cells does not prevent the cell from osmotic lysis. Animal cells do not have a cell wall like plant cells, and they are susceptible to osmotic lysis in a hypotonic environment where water can enter the cell freely, causing it to swell and potentially burst.

questions.text: Which of the following acts as a receptor site on the plasma membrane?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The heads of phospholipid molecules are hydrophilic (water-attracting) region that faces the aqueous environment on both sides of the cell membrane. While it plays a role in forming the lipid bilayer and contributes to the structure of the plasma membrane, it does not act as a receptor site. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Cholesterol molecules are an essential component of the plasma membrane, contributing to its fluidity and stability. However, cholesterol does not typically act as a receptor site on the plasma membrane. Therefore, this optionis also incorrect.

The tails of phospholipid molecules are hydrophobic (water-repelling) region that faces inward and is embedded within the lipid bilayer. Like the head, it contributes to the membrane's structure and plays a role in forming the lipid bilayer, but it does not act as a receptor site. So, this option is incorrect.

This option is correct. Glycoproteins are proteins that have carbohydrate chains (oligosaccharides) attached to them. These carbohydrate chains extend outward from the plasma membrane into the extracellular space. Glycoproteins often act as receptors on the cell surface, allowing the cell to recognize and interact with specific molecules, such as hormones, growth factors, or signaling molecules.

questions.explanation: Glycoproteins are proteins that have carbohydrate chains (oligosaccharides) attached to them. These carbohydrate chains extend outward from the plasma membrane into the extracellular space. Glycoproteins often act as receptors on the cell surface, allowing the cell to recognize and interact with specific molecules, such as hormones, growth factors, or signaling molecules.

questions.text: Which of the following best describes the function of RER in eukaryotic cells?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Glycosylation is the process by which a carbohydrate is covalently attached to a protein. This modification serves various functions. For instance, some proteins do not fold correctly unless they are glycosylated. In other cases, proteins are not stable unless they contain oligosaccharides linked at the amide nitrogen of certain asparagine residues. This option is incorrect as glycosylation is the function of the Golgi apparatus and not of RER.

The synthesis of lipids is the process by which cells create lipids from simpler molecules. Lipids are a diverse group of molecules that play important roles in cell structure, energy storage, and signaling. The synthesis of lipids occurs in the smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) of cells. So, this option is incorrect.

This option is the correct answer. The RER is the site of protein synthesis in eukaryotic cells. Ribosomes, which are the protein-making machines of the cell, are attached to the surface of the RER. When a ribosome binds to the RER, it begins to translate an mRNA transcript into a polypeptide chain, thus synthesizing proteins.

The synthesis of ribosomes is the process by which ribosomes are assembled in the cell. Ribosomes are essential for protein synthesis, and they are found in all living cells. The synthesis of ribosomes is a complex process that involves the coordinated transcription and translation of multiple genes. It occurs in the nucleolus and not in RER. So, this option is also incorrect.

questions.explanation: The RER is the site of protein synthesis in eukaryotic cells. Ribosomes, which are the protein-making machines of the cell, are attached to the surface of the RER. When a ribosome binds to the RER, it begins to translate an mRNA transcript into a polypeptide chain, thus synthesizing proteins.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is not true about the endoplasmic reticulum?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: It helps in the transport of proteins between various regions of cytoplasm.
options.explanation: This option is not the right answer. The endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is a network of membranes that helps to transport proteins between various regions of the cytoplasm. Proteins that are synthesized on ribosomes attached to the ER are transported to the Golgi apparatus, where they are further processed and sorted for delivery to their final destinations. The ER also helps to transport proteins between the cytoplasm and the nucleus. This is important for a variety of cellular processes, such as protein synthesis, DNA repair, and cell signaling.

options.text: It forms cytoplasmic framework for biochemical activities.
options.explanation: This option is also not the right answer because the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is a network of membrane-bound sacs and tubules that forms a cytoplasmic framework in eukaryotic cells. The ER provides a surface for a variety of biochemical activities, including:Protein synthesis: The ER is the site of protein synthesis in eukaryotic cells. Ribosomes, which are the protein-making machines of the cell, are attached to the surface of the ER. When a ribosome binds to the ER, it begins to translate an mRNA transcript into a polypeptide chain.Post-translational modifications: After a polypeptide chain is synthesized, it undergoes a series of post-translational modifications in the ER. These modifications include the addition of amino acids, sugars, and other molecules to the protein. These modifications help to fold the protein into its correct shape and make it functional etc.

options.text: It is involved in the formation of lysosomes.
options.explanation: This is not the right choice because the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is involved in the formation of lysosomes. The ER synthesizes the enzymes that are found in lysosomes, and it also helps to package these enzymes into vesicles that bud off from the ER. These vesicles then fuse with other vesicles from the Golgi apparatus to form lysosomes. The ER synthesizes the enzymes that are found in lysosomes. These enzymes are called hydrolases, and they are responsible for breaking down macromolecules into their constituent parts. The ER packages the hydrolases into vesicles that bud off from the ER. These vesicles are called transport vesicles. The transport vesicles fuse with other vesicles from the Golgi apparatus. The Golgi apparatus is responsible for modifying and sorting proteins, and it also packages some proteins into vesicles. The fusion of the transport vesicles from the ER and the Golgi apparatus forms lysosomes.

options.text: It is involved in the formation of cell wall.
options.explanation: This option is the right answer because the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is not involved in the formation of the cell wall. The cell wall is a rigid structure that surrounds the plasma membrane of plant cells. It is made up of cellulose, hemicellulose, and pectin. The ER is not involved in the synthesis of these molecules.

questions.explanation: The endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is not involved in the formation of the cell wall. The cell wall is a rigid structure that surrounds the plasma membrane of plant cells. It is made up of cellulose, hemicellulose, and pectin. The ER is not involved in the synthesis of these molecules.

questions.text: Which function is not related to the smooth endoplasmic reticulum?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is not the right answer because the smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) is involved in calcium storage. The SER is a network of tubules and vesicles that is found in the cytoplasm of cells. It is not studded with ribosomes, like the rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER), which gives it a smooth appearance. The SER is involved in a variety of functions, including lipid synthesis, detoxification, and calcium storage. Calcium is an important ion that plays a role in many cellular processes, including muscle contraction, cell signaling, and gene expression. The SER stores calcium in a highly concentrated form. This allows cells to quickly release calcium when needed, such as during muscle contraction.

This option is also not the right answer. The smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) is involved in the synthesis of steroids. Steroids are a class of lipids that play important roles in the body, including regulating metabolism, reproduction, and inflammation. questions.text: All of the following organelles contain DNA except:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct. Ribosomes do not contain DNA. Ribosomes are composed of RNA and proteins, while DNA is only found in the nucleus, mitochondria, and chloroplasts of a cell. Ribosomes are the site of protein synthesis in cells. They are made up of two subunits, a large subunit and a small subunit. The large subunit contains the mRNA binding site, while the small subunit contains the amino acid binding site. The RNA in ribosomes is called ribosomal RNA (rRNA). rRNA is responsible for the structure and function of ribosomes. It helps to bind the mRNA and the amino acids together, and it also helps to catalyze the reactions that are involved in protein synthesis.

This option is incorrect because chloroplasts contain DNA. Chloroplasts are organelles found in plant cells that are responsible for photosynthesis. They contain their own DNA, which is separate from the DNA in the nucleus of the cell. Chloroplast DNA is a circular molecule that is about 120,000 to 247,000 nucleotides in size. It encodes about 123 genes, including genes for the proteins that are involved in photosynthesis.

This option is also incorrect because the nucleus contains DNA. The nucleus is a membrane-bound organelle found in eukaryotic cells. It contains the cell's genetic material or DNA. DNA is the blueprint for the cell, and it contains the instructions for how to make proteins. The DNA in the nucleus is organized into chromosomes. Chromosomes are long, linear molecules of DNA that are tightly packaged. There are 23 pairs of chromosomes in human cells, for a total of 46 chromosomes.

This option is also incorrect because mitochondria contain DNA. Mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) is a circular molecule that is about 16,569 nucleotides in size. It encodes about 37 genes, including genes for the proteins that are involved in oxidative phosphorylation. Mitochondria are organelles found in all eukaryotic cells that are responsible for producing ATP, the cell's main source of energy. They contain their own DNA, which is separate from the DNA in the nucleus of the cell.

questions.text: Identify non-membranous organelle from the following:

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is a membranous organelle, which means that it is surrounded by a membrane. This option is correct. The centrosome is a non-membranous organelle found in animal cells. It is involved in the organization of the microtubules, which are the structures that form the mitotic spindle during cell division. The centrosome is composed of two centrioles, which are cylindrical structures that are arranged perpendicular to each other. The centrioles are surrounded by a cloud of amorphous material called the pericentriolar material (PCM). The centrosome is not surrounded by a membrane, which means that it is not technically an organelle. However, it is still considered to be an important organelle because it plays a vital role in cell division.

This option is incorrect because lysosomes are surrounded by a single membrane, which separates their contents from the cytoplasm of the cell. The lysosomal membrane is important for protecting the cell from the harmful enzymes that are found inside the lysosome. The membrane also helps to control the release of the enzymes into the cytoplasm.

questions.text: Modification of proteins and lipids into glycoproteins and glycolipids is the function of which organelle?

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the modification of proteins and lipids into glycoproteins and glycolipids is not the function of mitochondria. Mitochondria are the organelles that produce ATP, the cell's main source of energy. They do not have the enzymes or other machinery necessary to modify proteins and lipids.

The modification of proteins and lipids into glycoproteins and glycolipids is not the function of the cell membrane. The cell membrane is responsible for a variety of functions, including: Protection: The cell membrane protects the cell from its environment. Transport: The cell membrane allows nutrients and other molecules to enter the cell and waste products to exit the cell. Signaling: The cell membrane receives signals from other cells and responds to those signals. Attachment: The cell membrane helps cells to attach to each other and to the extracellular matrix.

This option is the right choice because the modification of proteins and lipids into glycoproteins and glycolipids is one of the functions of the Golgi apparatus. The Golgi apparatus is an organelle found in eukaryotic cells that is responsible for processing and sorting proteins and lipids. The Golgi apparatus is divided into three main regions: the cis-Golgi, the medial-Golgi, and the trans-Golgi. The cis-Golgi is the region where proteins and lipids are imported from the endoplasmic reticulum. The medial Golgi is the region where proteins and lipids are modified. The trans-Golgi is the region where proteins and lipids are sorted and exported from the Golgi apparatus. The modification of proteins and lipids in the Golgi apparatus involves a variety of processes, including: Glycosylation: The addition of sugar molecules to proteins and lipids. Phosphorylation: The addition of phosphate groups to proteins. Adenylation: The addition of adenosine molecules to proteins. Proteolytic cleavage: The removal of amino acids from proteins. The modification of proteins and lipids in the Golgi apparatus is essential for the proper functioning of cells. For example, glycoproteins are important for cell-cell recognition and adhesion. Glycolipids are important for the formation of cell membranes.

questions.text: The transport of secretory proteins takes place through organelles in which of the following order?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The transport of secretory proteins takes place through the following organelles in this order:Rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER): The RER is where secretory proteins are synthesized. The proteins are transported to the Golgi apparatus in vesicles that bud off from the RER. In the Golgi apparatus, the proteins are modified by the addition of carbohydrates and other molecules. They are also sorted and packaged into vesicles that will be transported to the cell surface.Secretory vesicles: The secretory vesicles are the final destination for secretory proteins. They bud off from the Golgi apparatus and travel to the cell surface. When the secretory vesicles reach the cell surface, they fuse with the plasma membrane and release the proteins into the extracellular space.So, the correct order for the transport of secretory proteins is RER → Golgi apparatus → secretory vesicles.

questions.text: Which of the following organelles is most abundant in those animal cells which exhibit phagocytic activity?

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the Golgi apparatus is not involved in phagocytic activity. Phagocytosis is the process by which cells engulf and destroy foreign particles or cells. It is a type of endocytosis, which is the process by which cells take in substances from the extracellular environment. Phagocytosis is carried out by specialized cells called phagocytes. Phagocytes have a number of unique features that allow them to engulf foreign particles. Phagocytes have lysosomes, which are organelles that contain enzymes that can break down foreign particles.

Cell walls are not found in animal cells. Only plant cells and some bacteria have cell walls. Animal cells do not have cell walls because they do not need them for support. Animal cells are supported by the extracellular matrix, which is a network of proteins and other molecules that surrounds the cell. Phagocytic cells are cells that can engulf and digest foreign particles. These cells include neutrophils, macrophages, and dendritic cells. Phagocytic cells do not have cell walls because they need to be able to move around and engulf particles. A cell wall would make it difficult for phagocytic cells to move and would also make it difficult for them to engulf particles.

This option is correct. Lysosomes are most abundant in those animal cells which exhibit phagocytic activity. Phagocytic cells are cells that engulf and destroy foreign particles, such as bacteria. Lysosomes are organelles that contain enzymes that break down cellular waste products and foreign substances. Phagocytic cells, such as macrophages and neutrophils, have a large number of lysosomes. This is because they need to be able to break down the foreign particles that they engulf. The lysosomes fuse with the phagosome, which is the vesicle that contains the foreign particle, and the enzymes in the lysosomes then break down the particle. The abundance of lysosomes in phagocytic cells is an important adaptation that allows these cells to defend the body against infection.

questions.text: Which of the following diseases is characterized by the accumulation of lipids in the brain cells leading to mental retardation and even death?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Graves' disease is an autoimmune disorder that causes the thyroid gland to produce too much thyroid hormone. The thyroid gland is a small, butterfly-shaped gland that is located in the neck. It produces thyroid hormones, which are essential for regulating metabolism, growth, and development.

This option is incorrect. Addison's disease is a rare disorder that occurs when the adrenal glands do not produce enough of the hormones cortisol and aldosterone. The adrenal glands are two small glands that sit on top of the kidneys. They produce a variety of hormones, including cortisol, aldosterone, and sex hormones.

This option is incorrect. Glycogen storage disease type II, also known as Pompe disease, is a rare, inherited disorder that affects the body's ability to break down glycogen, a type of sugar that is stored in the liver and muscles. In people with Pompe disease, the enzyme acid maltase is missing or not working properly. This enzyme is responsible for breaking down glycogen into glucose, which can be used for energy. Without acid maltase, glycogen builds up in the liver and muscles, causing a number of problems.

This option is correct. Tay-Sachs disease is a rare, inherited disorder that is caused by the accumulation of lipids or fatty substances called GM2 ganglioside in the brain cells. This accumulation of GM2 ganglioside leads to the death of brain cells, which results in progressive neurological decline. Tay-Sachs disease is usually diagnosed in infants and young children. The first symptoms of the disease typically appear between 4 and 6 months of age. These symptoms can include: Vision problems: Infants with Tay-Sachs disease may develop nystagmus, which is a condition in which the eyes make involuntary movements. They may also develop cataracts, which are cloudy areas in the lens of the eye. Developmental delays: Infants with Tay-Sachs disease may have delayed development. They may not reach their developmental milestones on time, such as rolling over, sitting up, or walking. Seizures: Infants with Tay-Sachs disease may develop seizures. Behavioral changes: Infants with Tay-Sachs disease may become irritable and withdrawn. They may also have difficulty sleeping.

questions.text: _ provides support for the individual plant cell and contributes to the turgidity of the leaves and younger parts of plants.

options.explanation: This answer is correct. Vacuoles are organelles found in plant cells that are responsible for storing a variety of substances, including water, nutrients, and waste products. They are also responsible for maintaining the turgidity of plant cells. Turgidity is the state of being firm and full. In plant cells, turgidity is caused by the pressure of the water in the vacuoles. When the vacuoles are full of water, they push against the cell wall, which makes the cell firm and rigid. The turgidity of plant cells is important for a number of reasons. It helps to maintain the shape of the cell and prevents it from collapsing. It also helps to transport water and nutrients throughout the plant. In addition to providing support, vacuoles also play a role in the storage of waste products. Some of the waste products that are stored in vacuoles include toxins, pigments, and salts.

In plant cells, turgidity is caused by the pressure of the water in the vacuoles. When the vacuoles are full of water, they push against the cell wall, which makes the cell firm and rigid. The turgidity of plant cells is important for a number of reasons. It helps to maintain the shape of the cell and prevents it from collapsing. It also helps to transport water and nutrients throughout the plant. In addition to providing support, vacuoles also play a role in the storage of waste products. Some of the waste products that are stored in vacuoles include toxins, pigments, and salts.

questions.explanation: Plastids are present in autotrophic eukaryotes and multicellular organisms such as plants and algae. Animals are heterotrophs and viruses are not considered as living organisms.

questions.text: What is the plasma membrane chemically composed of?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Phospholipids are a key component of the plasma membrane. They are amphipathic molecules, meaning they have both hydrophilic (water-loving) and hydrophobic (water-repelling) regions. Phospholipids form a lipid bilayer structure in the plasma membrane, with their hydrophilic heads facing outward and their hydrophobic tails facing inward, creating a barrier.

The plasma membrane is composed of lipids and proteins. In addition to phospholipids, the membrane contains other lipid molecules, such as cholesterol, which helps regulate membrane fluidity. Proteins are embedded within the lipid bilayer or attached to its surfaces.

While lipids are an essential component of the plasma membrane, carbohydrates are not a major chemical component. However, there are glycolipids present in the plasma membrane. Glycolipids are lipids with attached carbohydrate groups.

Glycolipids are lipids with attached carbohydrate groups. They are present in the plasma membrane and play important roles in cell recognition, cell adhesion, and cell signaling. Glycolipids are involved in interactions between cells and their environment and are crucial for cell communication.

questions.explanation: The plasma membrane is made up 60-80 % of proteins, 20-40 % of lipids and a very small amount of carbohydrates.

questions.text: The endoplasmic reticulum contains a system of flattened membrane-bounded sacs which are called?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cristae are not found in the endoplasmic reticulum. Cristae are infoldings of the inner mitochondrial membrane, not the endoplasmic reticulum. They provide a larger surface area for chemical reactions involved in cellular respiration.
Marks is not a term used to describe the structure of the endoplasmic reticulum. It does not refer to any specific component or feature of the endoplasmic reticulum.

Cisternae are the correct answer. The endoplasmic reticulum contains a system of flattened, membrane-bounded sacs called cisternae. These cisternae are interconnected and form a network within the cell. The endoplasmic reticulum plays a vital role in protein and lipid synthesis, as well as the transport of molecules within the cell.

While the endoplasmic reticulum does have tubular regions, the main feature being described in the question is the flattened membrane-bounded sacs called cisternae. Tubules are smaller, more elongated structures within the endoplasmic reticulum network.

questions.explanation: The material in the Endoplasmic reticulum is separated from the cytoplasmic materials by spherical or tubular membranes called cisternae.

questions.text: Which organelle synthesizes enzymes?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mitochondria are often referred to as the "powerhouses" of the cell because they produce energy in the form of ATP through cellular respiration. While mitochondria do contain enzymes involved in energy production, they are not primarily responsible for synthesizing enzymes.

Vacuoles are membrane-bound organelles that have various functions depending on the cell type. They primarily function in storage, waste disposal, and maintaining turgor pressure in plant cells. Vacuoles are not directly involved in enzyme synthesis.

The rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) is responsible for protein synthesis and plays a major role in synthesizing enzymes. It contains ribosomes attached to its surface, which are involved in protein synthesis. The RER is abundant in cells that produce large amounts of proteins, such as enzymes.

Ribosomes are cellular structures responsible for protein synthesis. They are not considered an organelle, but rather a complex of RNA and proteins. Ribosomes can be found in both the cytoplasm and on the rough endoplasmic reticulum. They are involved in the synthesis of all types of proteins, including enzymes.

questions.explanation: Most of the enzymes are protein molecules and are synthesized on the ribosomes.

questions.text: Ribosomes exist in two forms. They are either attached with the RER or freely dispersed in the _
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The tonoplast is a membrane that surrounds the central vacuole in plant cells. It is involved in maintaining the osmotic balance and storage of various substances within the vacuole. Ribosomes are not typically associated with the tonoplast.

The Golgi apparatus, also known as the Golgi complex or Golgi bodies, is involved in processing, modifying, and sorting proteins and lipids received from the endoplasmic reticulum. While the Golgi apparatus plays a role in protein trafficking, it is not directly associated with the presence of ribosomes.

The cytoplasm is the fluid-filled region inside the cell that surrounds the organelles. Ribosomes are indeed freely dispersed in the cytoplasm. These ribosomes are responsible for synthesizing proteins that are not destined for insertion into the endoplasmic reticulum.

The SER stands for smooth endoplasmic reticulum. It is a network of membranous tubules involved in various cellular processes, such as lipid metabolism, detoxification, and calcium storage. Ribosomes are not typically associated with the smooth endoplasmic reticulum. They are primarily found on the rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER).

questions.explanation: Therefore, the correct answer is C) Cytoplasm, as ribosomes can exist freely dispersed in the cytoplasm when not attached to the rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER).

questions.text: The ribosomal RNA is synthesized and stored in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The rRNA is synthesized and stored in the nucleolus. Central fibril area of the nucleolus has large molecular weight RNA and rDNA.
The rRNA is synthesized and stored in the nucleolus. Central fibril area of the nucleolus has large molecular weight RNA and rDNA.
The rRNA is synthesized and stored in the nucleolus. Central fibril area of the nucleolus has large molecular weight RNA and rDNA.
The rRNA is synthesized and stored in the nucleolus. Central fibril area of the nucleolus has large molecular weight RNA and rDNA.

questions.explanation: The rRNA is synthesized and stored in the nucleolus. Central fibril area of the nucleolus has large molecular weight RNA and rDNA.

questions.text: Which model of the plasma membrane suggests that proteins are embedded in lipid bilayer?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The unit membrane model is not specifically associated with the suggestion that proteins are embedded in the lipid bilayer of the plasma membrane. The unit membrane model refers to the general structural organization of cellular membranes, including the plasma membrane, which consists of a lipid bilayer with embedded proteins.

The fluid mosaic model is the correct answer. It suggests that proteins are embedded in the lipid bilayer of the plasma membrane. According to this model, the plasma membrane is composed of a fluid lipid bilayer in which various proteins are embedded, giving it a mosaic-like appearance. These proteins can span the entire lipid bilayer (integral proteins) or be associated with either the inner or outer surface of the membrane (peripheral proteins).

The term "permeable" refers to the property of allowing substances to pass through. While permeability is a characteristic of the plasma membrane, it does not specifically address the suggestion that proteins are embedded in the lipid bilayer.

An ultracentrifuge is a laboratory instrument used to separate particles based on their density using high-speed centrifugal force. While ultracentrifuges are valuable tools in cell biology research, they are not directly related to the suggestion that proteins are embedded in the lipid bilayer of the plasma membrane.

questions.explanation: Therefore, the correct answer is B) Fluid Mosaic, which suggests that proteins are embedded in the lipid bilayer of the plasma membrane.

questions.text: Lipid metabolism is the function of which organelle?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mitochondria are involved in several functions, including energy production through cellular respiration. While mitochondria do play a role in lipid metabolism by participating in the beta-oxidation of fatty acids, they are not the primary organelles responsible for lipid metabolism.

The sarcoplasmic reticulum is a specialized type of endoplasmic reticulum found in muscle cells. It is primarily responsible for storing and releasing calcium ions during muscle contraction. Lipid metabolism is not a primary function of the sarcoplasmic reticulum.

The rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) is involved in protein synthesis and processing. While it plays a role in lipid metabolism by participating in the synthesis of certain lipids, it is not the primary organelle responsible for overall lipid metabolism.

The smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) is the correct answer. It is primarily responsible for lipid metabolism. The SER is involved in the synthesis of lipids, such as phospholipids and steroids, and the detoxification and metabolism of lipids and other molecules. It also plays a role in calcium storage and release in certain cell types.

questions.explanation: Therefore, the correct answer is D) SER (Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum), which is the organelle primarily responsible for lipid metabolism.

questions.text: The enzymes of lysosomes are synthesized in the _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) is involved in protein synthesis and processing. It contains ribosomes attached to its surface, which are responsible for synthesizing proteins. However, the enzymes of lysosomes are not synthesized in the RER. The RER is primarily associated with the synthesis of proteins destined for secretion or insertion into the cell membrane.

The smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) is involved in various functions, including lipid metabolism, detoxification, and calcium storage. However, the synthesis of enzymes for lysosomes does not occur in the SER. The SER is primarily associated with lipid metabolism and does not have a direct role in lysosomal enzyme synthesis.

Chloroplasts are organelles found in plant cells and some algae that are responsible for photosynthesis. They contain chlorophyll and are involved in converting light energy into chemical energy. Chloroplasts are not involved in the synthesis of lysosomal enzymes.

The Golgi apparatus, also known as the Golgi complex, is responsible for modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins and lipids received from the endoplasmic reticulum. The enzymes of lysosomes are synthesized in the rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) and then transported to the Golgi apparatus for further processing and packaging into vesicles. These vesicles are then transported to lysosomes.

questions.explanation: Therefore, the correct answer is A) RER, as it is the organelle where lysosomal enzymes are synthesized and processed before being transported to lysosomes.

questions.text: The process by which unwanted structures within the cell are engulfed and digested within the lysosome is known as _?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Endocytosis is the process by which cells take in external materials by engulfing them into vesicles formed from the plasma membrane. While endocytosis involves the uptake of materials into the cell, it does not specifically refer to the digestion of unwanted structures within the lysosome.

Exocytosis is the process by which materials enclosed in vesicles are released from the cell into the extracellular space. It is the opposite of endocytosis. Exocytosis is not specifically related to the digestion of unwanted structures within the lysosome.

Hydrolysis is a chemical reaction in which a compound is broken down by the addition of water. While hydrolysis does occur within the lysosome during the digestion process, it does not specifically describe the overall process of engulfing and digesting unwanted structures within the cell.

The correct answer is D) Autophagy. Autophagy is the process by which unwanted or damaged cellular components, such as organelles or proteins, are engulfed within a double-membraned structure called an autophagosome. The autophagosome then fuses with a lysosome, where the contents are degraded and recycled.

questions.explanation: Therefore, the correct answer is D) Autophagy, which specifically refers to the process by which unwanted structures within the cell are engulfed and digested within the lysosome.

questions.text: The outer membrane of the nuclear envelope is at places continuous with the _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The outer membrane of the nuclear envelope is not directly continuous with the Golgi apparatus. While both the nuclear envelope and the Golgi apparatus are membrane-bound structures within the cell, they are distinct and separate entities. The Golgi apparatus is involved in modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins and lipids received from the endoplasmic reticulum.

The correct answer is B) Endoplasmic Reticulum. The outer membrane of the nuclear envelope is continuous with the endoplasmic reticulum (ER). The ER is a network of membranous tubules involved in protein synthesis, lipid metabolism, and other cellular processes.

Lysozymes, also known as lysozymes or lytic enzymes, are enzymes found within the lysosomes. They are responsible for breaking down various molecules, including certain polysaccharides and bacteria. The outer membrane of the nuclear envelope is not directly continuous with lysozymes.

Peroxisomes are membrane-bound organelles involved in various metabolic reactions, such as lipid metabolism and detoxification of harmful substances. While peroxisomes may interact with other cellular components, the outer membrane of the nuclear envelope is not directly continuous with peroxisomes.

questions.explanation: Therefore, the correct answer is B) Endoplasmic Reticulum, as the outer membrane of the nuclear envelope is continuous with the endoplasmic reticulum.

questions.text: The function of nucleolus is to make _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The nucleolus is involved in the synthesis and assembly of ribosomes, but it does not directly make rDNA. rDNA refers to the genes that encode the RNA components of ribosomes, specifically the ribosomal RNA (rRNA). These genes are located in the genome of an organism. The nucleolus plays a role in processing and assembling the rRNA molecules transcribed from the rDNA.

The correct answer is B) Ribosomes. The function of the nucleolus is to make ribosomes. Ribosomes are cellular structures involved in protein synthesis. The nucleolus houses the genes that encode the RNA components of ribosomes (rRNA), synthesizes and assembles the rRNA, and combines it with ribosomal proteins to form functional ribosomes.

The nucleolus is directly involved in the synthesis and processing of RNA, including the production of ribosomal RNA (rRNA). rRNA is a type of RNA that is a crucial component of ribosomes. However, the nucleolus does not exclusively make RNA. It specifically plays a significant role in the production of rRNA.

The nucleolus is not primarily involved in making chromosomes. Chromosomes are condensed structures of DNA that contain genes. The nucleolus is a distinct region within the nucleus and is primarily associated with the synthesis and assembly of ribosomes.

questions.explanation: Therefore, the correct answer is B) Ribosomes, as the main function of the nucleolus is to make ribosomes.

questions.text: This cell which is the longest in the human body:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Muscle cells, also known as muscle fibers, are responsible for the contraction and movement of muscles in the body. While muscle cells can vary in length depending on the muscle they belong to, they are generally not as long as nerve cells. Muscle fibers are elongated cells that can span from a few millimeters to several centimeters in length. They contain specialized structures called myofibrils, which are responsible for the contractile properties of muscle tissue.

Nerve cells, or neurons, are specialized cells of the nervous system that transmit electrical signals throughout the body. Neurons consist of a cell body, dendrites (short branched extensions that receive signals from other neurons), and an elongated projection called an axon. It is the axon that can be exceptionally long, allowing for communication over long distances. For example, some neurons in the spinal cord extend all the way to the toes or fingertips, making them the longest cells in the human body.

Bone cells, also known as osteocytes, are the primary cells found in bone tissue. While bone cells play a vital role in maintaining bone health and remodeling, they are not typically characterized by their length. Bone tissue itself can be quite long, as it forms the structural framework of the skeletal system, but individual bone cells do not exhibit significant elongation compared to nerve cells.

Gland cells are found in various glands of the body and are responsible for producing and secreting substances such as hormones, enzymes, or mucus. Gland cells can have different shapes and sizes depending on the gland they belong to, but they are generally not known for their exceptional length. Their function primarily involves the production and release of specific substances rather than elongated structures.

questions.explanation: The nerve cells or neurons are the longest cells in the human body with some neurons being as long as 1-1.5 meters. These cells are responsible for transmitting signals throughout the body

The nucleus is the control center of the cell and contains the cell's genetic material. While the nucleus plays a crucial role in gene expression and regulation, it is not directly involved in ATP synthesis.
Centrioles are cylindrical structures involved in cell division, specifically in the organization of spindle fibers. However, they are not directly involved in ATP synthesis.

questions.explanation: Therefore, the correct answer is B) Mitochondria, as they are the organelles primarily involved in ATP synthesis within the cell.

questions.text: Which organelle is involved in aerobic respiration?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mitochondria are the powerhouses of the cell, and they play a critical role in aerobic respiration. Aerobic respiration is a process that cells use to break down glucose and other nutrients in the presence of oxygen to produce ATP, the cell's main source of energy. The mitochondria are responsible for producing most of the ATP in the cell. They do this by using the energy from the electrons carried by NADH and FADH2 to pump protons across the inner mitochondrial membrane. This creates an electrochemical gradient, which is used by ATP synthase to produce ATP.
Lysosomes are organelles that contain enzymes that break down cellular waste. They do not participate in the breakdown of glucose or the production of ATP.

Plastids are organelles that are found in plant cells. They contain chlorophyll and are responsible for photosynthesis. Photosynthesis is a process that uses sunlight to convert carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen. Glucose can then be used for aerobic respiration, but the plastids themselves do not participate in this process.
Ribosomes are organelles that synthesize proteins. They do not participate in the breakdown of glucose or the production of ATP.

questions.explanation: Mitochondria are the powerhouses of the cell, and they play a critical role in aerobic respiration. Aerobic respiration is a process that cells use to break down glucose and other nutrients in the presence of oxygen to produce ATP, the cell's main source of energy. The mitochondria are responsible for producing most of the ATP in the cell. They do this by using the energy from the electrons carried by NADH and FADH2 to pump protons across the inner mitochondrial membrane. This creates an electrochemical gradient, which is used by ATP synthase to produce ATP.

questions.text: What is the inner membrane of the mitochondria called?

options.explanation: A cisterna is a flattened membrane vesicle found in the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) and Golgi apparatus. Cisternae are an integral part of the packaging and modification processes of proteins occurring in the Golgi.
Cristae are the folds of the inner membrane of mitochondria.The inner membrane of mitochondria is where most of the cellular respiration takes place.

Lemma is a specialized bract that surrounds the florets in grasses. It is a modified leaf that is usually larger and tougher than the other bracts. The lemma protects the florets from the elements and helps to keep them in place.

A tonoplast is the membrane that surrounds the vacuole in plant cells. It is a single layer of phospholipids, just like the cell membrane, but it has different proteins embedded in it. These proteins allow the tonoplast to control the movement of ions and other molecules into and out of the vacuole.

questions.explanation: Cristae are the folds of the inner membrane of mitochondria.The inner membrane of mitochondria is where most of the cellular respiration takes place. The cristae increase the surface area of the inner membrane, which allows for more efficient cellular respiration. This is because the cristae provide more space for the proteins and enzymes that are involved in cellular respiration.In addition to increasing the surface area of the inner membrane, the cristae also help to anchor the proteins and enzymes that are involved in cellular respiration. This helps to keep the proteins and enzymes in the correct position so that they can function properly.

questions.text: The organelle serving as a primary packaging area for molecules that will be distributed throughout the cell is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Vacuole is a membrane-bound sac that is found in plant cells and some animal cells.It is used for storage.

Plastids are found in plant cells and some algae. They are responsible for photosynthesis, which is the process by which plants convert sunlight into energy. Plastids come in different types, including chloroplasts, chromoplasts, and leucoplasts.

Mitochondria are double-membraned organelles that are found in most eukaryotic cells. They are responsible for producing ATP, the cell's main source of energy. Mitochondria are often called the "powerhouses" of the cell. The inner membrane of mitochondria is folded into cristae, which increase the surface area of the membrane. This allows for more efficient cellular respiration.

Golgi body is a flattened organelle that is found in most eukaryotic cells. It is responsible for packaging and modifying proteins, and it also plays a role in the transport of proteins to other parts of the cell. The Golgi body is made up of a stack of flattened sacs called cisternae. The proteins that are transported to the Golgi body from the endoplasmic reticulum are sorted into different cisternae based on their destination. The proteins that are destined for secretion are packaged into vesicles that bud off from the Golgi body. The vesicles then travel to the cell membrane and fuse with it, releasing the proteins into the extracellular space.

questions.explanation: The Golgi body is served as a 'primary packaging area' for molecules that will be 'distributed throughout the cell".It is a flattened organelle that is found in most eukaryotic cells. It is responsible for packaging and modifying proteins, and it also plays a role in the transport of proteins to other parts of the cell. The Golgi body is made up of a stack of flattened sacs called cisternae. The proteins that are transported to the Golgi body from the endoplasmic reticulum are sorted into different cisternae based on their destination. The proteins that are destined for secretion are packaged into vesicles that bud off from the Golgi body. The vesicles then travel to the cell membrane and fuse with it, releasing the proteins into the extracellular space.

questions.text: The Cell theory is not applicable to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Fungi are eukaryotic organisms, meaning that their cells have a nucleus and other membrane-bound organelles. The cells of fungi are typically larger than the cells of bacteria, and they have a more complex structure.
The cell theory is applicable to algae because algae are eukaryotic organisms, which means that their cells have a nucleus and other membrane-bound organelles.

Here are some of the reasons why the cell theory is not applicable to viruses:Viruses do not have a cell membrane. A cell membrane is a structure that surrounds all cells and provides protection and support. Viruses do not have a cell membrane, and they are instead surrounded by a protein coat.Viruses do not have their own metabolism. Metabolism is the process by which cells convert energy and nutrients into molecules that the cell can use. Viruses do not have their own metabolism, and they rely on the host cell to provide them with the energy and nutrients they need to survive.Viruses do not reproduce on their own. Cells can reproduce by dividing in half. Viruses cannot reproduce on their own, and they need to infect a host cell in order to reproduce.
Microbes are a diverse group of organisms that includes bacteria, archaea, and protozoa. The cells of microbes are typically very small, and they can be found in a variety of environments.

questions.text: Which organelle does the tonoplast surround?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The Golgi complex is a stack of flattened sacs that is found in the cytoplasm of cells. The Golgi complex is involved in the processing and packaging of proteins and lipids.
The nucleus is a membrane-bound organelle that contains the cell's DNA. The nucleus is responsible for storing genetic information and controlling the cell's activities.

The endoplasmic reticulum is a network of tubules and sacs that is found in the cytoplasm of cells. The ER is involved in a variety of functions, including protein synthesis, lipid synthesis, and detoxification. The ER membrane is made up of two layers of phospholipids, with proteins embedded in the bilayer. The proteins in the ER membrane provide structure and function to the organelle.

The tonoplast is the membrane that surrounds the vacuole in plant cells. It is a single membrane that is made up of phospholipids and proteins. The proteins in the tonoplast membrane are involved in a variety of functions, including transport of ions and molecules into and out of the vacuole, storage of water and turgor pressure.

questions.explanation: Vacuole is single membrane bounded organelle. This single membrane is known as tonoplast. The tonoplast is the membrane that surrounds the vacuole in plant cells. It is a single membrane that is made up of phospholipids and proteins. The proteins in the tonoplast membrane are involved in a variety of functions, including: Transport of ions and molecules into and out of the vacuole: The tonoplast membrane is selectively permeable, which means that it allows some molecules to pass through it while blocking others. This selective permeability is important for maintaining the concentration of ions and molecules inside the vacuole.Storage of water: The vacuole is a large organelle that stores water. The tonoplast membrane helps to regulate the amount of water in the vacuole by controlling the movement of water in and out of the vacuole.Turgor pressure: Turgor pressure is the pressure that is exerted by the water in the vacuole against the cell wall. The tonoplast membrane helps to maintain turgor pressure by regulating the amount of water in the vacuole.
