It is caused due to deficiency of enzyme related with lipid catabolism (break down).
It is caused due to deficiency of enzyme related with lipid catabolism (break down).
It is caused due to deficiency of enzyme related with lipid catabolism (break down).

questions.explanation: Glyoxisomes contain such enzymes which are associated with conversion of stored fatty acids to carbohydrates in seedlings of oil yielding plants.

questions.text: Following organelles are derived from special assemblies of microtubules, EXCEPT:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is not the right choice. Cilia are hair-like projections on the surface of eukaryotic cells. They are derived from special assemblies of microtubules.
This option is incorrect. Flagella are whip-like appendages that protrudes from certain cells. They are also derived from special assemblies of microtubules.
This option is incorrect because centrioles are also derived from microtubules. Each centriole is a cylindrical array of nine microtubules.
This is the correct answer. Microbodies are usually membrane-bounded vesicular structures present in the cell's cytoplasm. They are not derived from microtubules.

questions.explanation: Microbodies are simple membrane-bounded vesicular organelles like peroxisomes and glyoxisomes.

questions.text: Cyclosis occurs due to the involvement of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Microtubules are involved in the formation of cilia, flagella, and centrioles. They are not concerned with cyclosis.
This is the correct answer. Mcrofilaments are slender cylinders made up of contractile actin protein. They are involved in internal cell motion such as cyclosis.
This option is incorrect. Intermediate filaments are involved in the determination of cell shape and the integration of cellular compartments. They are not concerned with cyclosis.
This option is incorrect. Myofilaments are contractile filaments present in muscle cells like cardiac and skeletal muscles. They are involved in the contraction of muscle cells.

questions.explanation: Microfilaments are made up of contractile actin proteins. They contract and relax rhythmically in a cell and generate a pumping pressure that forces the cytoplasm to carry out a mass movementor streaming movement in a clockwise manner around the nucleus. It is called cyclosis.

questions.text: Intermediate filaments are called so because they have intermediate:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Intermediate means in the middle. Intermediate filaments are not present in the middle of the cell. Instead, they form an elaborate network extending from the ring surrounding the nucleus to the plasma membrane.

This option is also incorrect. Intermediate composition means that they have a composition in between the composition of microtubules and microfilaments. However, this is not true for intermediate filaments.
This option is also incorrect because the length of intermediate filaments is not in between the length of microfilaments and microtubules.
This is the correct answer. Intermediate filaments have diameter in between those of microtubules and microfilaments.

questions.explanation: Fact: The diameter of intermediate filaments is in between that of microtubules and microfilaments.

questions.text: Absence of an enzyme that is involved in the catabolism of lipids may ultimately result in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Glycogenosis is a disease caused by the deficiency of enzyme involved in the metabolism of glycogen and glucose.

This option is inappropriate because all the diseases caused by the mutations in lysosomal enzymes are collectively called lysosomal disorders. Specifically, the disease caused by the absence of lipid-digesting enzymes is called Tay-Sach's disease which can cause mental retardation and even death.

This is the correct answer. If a lysosomal enzyme, that is involved in the catabolism of ipids, is absent, the accumulation of lipids in the brain cells can occur. It causes mental retardation and eventually death.
This option is also inappropriate. 'Storage diseases' is a collective name for the diseases that occur due to the accumulation of certain substances in the cell.

questions.explanation: Due to the deficiency of such enzymes, lipids are accumulated in the brain cells, which lead to mental retardation or even death. The disorder is called Tay Sach’s disease.

questions.text: The most undesirable consequence of Tay Sach's disease is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Accumulation of lipids in brain cells
options.explanation: This option is inappropriate. Accumulation of lipids is not undesirable until it causes any harmful effect.
This option is incorrect because mental retardation is not the most undesirable consequence. Death is the most undesirable effect of any disease.
This is the correct option. Death is the most undesirable consequence of any disease.

options.text: Absence of an enzyme involved in catabolism of lipids
options.explanation: This option is also incorrect. The absence of enzyme is not undesirable until it causes serious harmful effects.

questions.explanation: The worst consequence of the accumulation of lipids in brain is the death of human being. Even with the best care, children with infantile Tay Sach’s disease usually die at the age of 4.

questions.text: Following events are associated with a lysosomal disorder called Tay Sach's disease. Arrange them in a specific sequence with respect to their occurrence: i. Mental retardation ii. Inheritance of abnormal gene iii. Accumulation of lipids in brain cells iv. Lack of catabolism of lipids v. Absence of an enzyme
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The correct sequence is ii, v, iv, iii, i.
The correct sequence is ii, v, iv, iii, i.
The correct sequence is ii, v, iv, iii, ii.

This is the correct sequence. First, an abnormal gene is inherited. Second, due to abnormal gene expression, a particular lysosomal enzyme is not produced. Then, due to the lack of that particular enzyme, lipids are not catabolized. This results in the accumulation of lipids in the brain cells, which causes mental retardation.

questions.explanation: When someone inherits an abnormal gene associated with the synthesis of the enzyme required for the catabolism of lipids, the person will lack such enzymes. As a consequence, there willbe no catabolism of lipids, and lipids will be accumulated in the brain cells. It will result in mental retardation.

questions.text: Events associated with lysosomal disorders are mentioned below. Arrange them in a sequence with respect to their occurrence: i. Mutation ii. Storage diseases iii. Accumulation of substances within the cells iv. Deficiency of lysosomal enzymes v. Metabolic sluggishness
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The correct sequence is i, iv, v, iii, ii.

This is the correct sequence. Mutations in the genes result in the lack of formation of a particular lysosomal enzyme. The absence of that enzyme disturbs the metabolism of a particular type of substance. As a result, that substance is accumulated in the cells. This leads to storage diseases.
The correct sequence is i, iv, v, iii, ii.
The correct sequence is i, iv, v, iii, ii.

questions.explanation: When the gene associated with enzyme synthesis is mutated, the enzyme becomes deficient, and as a result, the metabolic process becomes sluggish, and the substrate (substance) starts to accumulate in the cells. This results in storage disease.

questions.text: Autophagy in a cell is carried out by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the correct option. Lysosomes are involved in a process called autophagy (self-eating). During this process, some old, worn-out parts of the cell, such as old mitochondria, are digested. In this way, the materials of the cell may be recycled, and the cell may be renewed.
This option is incorrect. The only function of peroxisomes is the formation and decomposition of hydrogen peroxide. They are not involved in autophagy.

This option is incorrect. Glyoxysomes contain enzymes, including glycolic acid oxidase and catalase. They are present in plant cells and are involved in the conversion of stored fatty acids to carbohydrates in young germinating seedlings. They are not associated with autophagy.
This option is incorrect. Mesosomes are present in prokaryotic cells. They are involved in cellular respiration and DNA replication. They are not concerned with autophagy.

questions.explanation: Lysosomes are involved in a process called autophagy (self-eating). During this process, some old, worn-out parts of the cell, such as old mitochondria, are digested. In this way, the materials of the cell may be recycled, and the cell may be renewed.

questions.text: Autophagy is carried out by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is an inappropriate option. Lysosomes that are involved in autophagy (self-eating) are specifically called autophagosomes. This option can be correct if autophagosome is not given in the options.

This is the right option. Lysosomes that are involved in autophagy (self-eating) are specifically called autophagosomes. During this process, some old, worn-out parts of the cell, such as old mitochondria, are digested. In this way, the materials of the cell may be recycled, and the cell may be renewed.

This option is incorrect. Heterophagosomes are formed by the fusion of food vacuole phagocytosed by the cell from outside. They are involved in the digestion of ingested food particles. On the other hand, autophagy is the digestion of a cell's own components.
This option is incorrect. The only function of peroxisomes is the formation and decomposition of hydrogen peroxide. They are not involved in autophagy.

questions.explanation: Lysosomes associated with self-eating (autophagy) are called autophagosomes.

questions.text: The organelle that protects the cells from invading organisms or any other foreign object is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the right answer. Pathogens often hijack endocytic pathways such as pinocytosis in order to gain entry into the cell. The lysosome prevents easy entry into the cell by hydrolyzing the biomolecules of pathogens necessary for their replication strategies.

This option is incorrect. Transport vesicles of ER are not functional until they are modified by Golgi apparatus and are budded off from Golgi apparatus as lysosomes. So, they cannot destroy the pathogens.
This option is inappropriate. Every Golgi vesicle may not necessarily contain digestive enzymes. Hence, every Golgi vesicle is not a lysosome that can destroy pathogens that get entry into the cell.

This option is incorrect. Autophagosomes are the lysosomes that engulf and digest the old, worn-out parts of the cell. They are not involved in the digestion of particles phagocytosed from the outside of the cell.

questions.explanation: Pathogens often hijack endocytic pathways such as pinocytosis in order to gain entry into the cell. The lysosome prevents easy entry into the cell by hydrolyzing the biomolecules of pathogens necessary for their replication strategies. Reduced lysosomal activity is an increase in viral infectivity.

questions.text: The proteins found in muscles are also found in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. Muscle cells are made up of actin, myosin,and tropomyosin proteins. These proteins are also found in the cytoskeleton of the cell.
This option is incorrect. The exoskeleton of most of the animals is made up of calcium salts or chitin. Muscle proteins like actin, myosin, and tropomyosin are not present in it.
This option is incorrect. The endoskeleton is composed of bones and cartilages, Both of them are connective tissues. They are not made up of actin or myosin proteins.
This option is incorrect. Cartilage is a dense connective tissue. It contains collagen protein. It does not contain muscle proteins.

questions.explanation: Actin, myosin, tropomyosin, and other proteins that are part of muscles are also part of the cytoskeleton.

questions.text: The cytoskeletal fabric consists of the following structural components EXCEPT:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is an incorrect option. Microtubules are present in the cytoskeletal fabric.
This is the correct answer. Cytosol is a part of the cytoplasm of the cell. It is not present in the cytoskeletal fabric.
This option is incorrect. Microfilaments are present in the cytokeletal fabric.
This option is incorrect because intermediate filaments are also present in the cytoskeletal fabric.

questions.explanation: Cytosol is the soluble or solution part of the cytoplasm, whereas the cytoskeleton consists of three components, i.e., microtubules, microfilaments, and intermediate filaments.

questions.text: Amoeboid movements are because of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Intermediate filaments are involved in the determination of cell shape and the integration of cellular compartments. Amoeboid movements are not caused by them.
This option is incorrect. Microtubules are involved in the formation of cilia, flagella, basal bodies, and centrioles.
This is the correct answer. The movement of cyclosis and amoeboid movements are caused by microfilaments.

This option in inappropriate. Amoeboid movements are caused by microfilaments. Although microfilaments are a part of cytoskeleton, but this can't be the right answer because microfilament is given in the options.

questions.explanation: Microfilaments are associated with streaming movement or mass movement of cytoplasm, called cyclosis. Any intracellular movement of cytoplasm is carried out by the rhythmic contraction of microfilaments, and for pseudopodial movement, intracellular movement of cytoplasm is required.

questions.text: Determination of cell shape and integration of cellular compartments is the role of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Microfilaments are involved in the movement of cyclosis and amoeboid movements. Determination of cell shape and integration of cellular compartments is not their function.
This option is incorrect. Cell membrane is very delicate and flexible. It does not determine the shape of the cell.
This is the correct option. Intermediate filaments are involved in the determination of cell shape and integration of cellular compartments.
This option is incorrect. Although cell wall determines the cell shape, but it is not present in all types of cell. Also, it is not involved in the integration of cellular compartments.

questions.explanation: Intermediate filaments are aggregated on the inner surface of the cell membrane and in the space between the cell membrane and the nucleus and provide support to the cell membrane to maintain its shape and the inner compartment of the cell.

questions.text: Most distinctive feature of prokaryotic cell is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because flagella is also found in some eukaryotic cells.
This option is incorrect. PProkaryotic cells donot lack ribosomes. They contain 70S ribosomes.

This is the correct answer. The absence of a nucleus is the most distinctive feature of prokaryotic cells. They do not contain membrane-bounded nuclei. Instead, their genetic material is dispersed in the cytoplasm.
This option is incorrect. Cytoplasm is present in all types of cell. It is not a distinctive feature of prokaryotic cells.

questions.explanation: The distinguishing features of prokaryotic cells is that they lack nucleus and membrane-bound organelles. All prokaryotic organisms are unicellular, made of only one cell.

questions.text: Proteins are converted into glycoproteins by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The only function of nucleolus is ribosome synthesis. Nucleoli are termed as ribosome factories.

This option is incorrect because mitochondria are involved in cellular respiration. They synthesize energy in the form of ATP for the cell. They are termed as powerhouses of the cell. They are not involved in the modification of proteins.

This is the correct answer. The most important function of the Golgi apparatus is the modification of proteins and lipids, which are synthesized by ER, into glycoproteins and glycolipids, respectively.

This option is incorrect. Endoplasmic reticulum is involved in the synthesis of proteins and lipids, but they are not involved in their modification. Their modification is the function of Golgi apparatus.

questions.explanation: The most important function of the Golgi apparatus is the modification of proteins and lipids. Lipids and proteins synthesized by ER are further modified by the Golgi apparatus into glycolipids and glycoproteins, respectively.

questions.text: They are organelles of symbiotic origin:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. According to the endosymbiotic hypothesis, mitochondria are thought to have a symbiotic origin, but the nucleus does not.
This option is incorrect. Neither the Golgi apparatus nor ER is thought to have a symbiotic origin according to the endosymbiotic hypothesis.

This is the correct answer. The eukaryotic cell might have evolved when a large anaerobic (living without oxygen) amoeboid prokaryote ingested small aerobic (living with oxygen) bacteria and stabilized them instead of digesting them. This idea is known as the endosymbiont hypothesis. According to this hypothesis, the aerobic bacteria developed into mitochondria, which are the sites of aerobic respiration. Ingestion of prokaryotes that resembled present-day cyanobacteria could have led to the endosymbiotic development of chloroplasts in plants.
This option is incorrect. The endosymbiotic hypothesis does not explain the evolution of cilia.

questions.explanation: The eukaryotic cell might have evolved when a large anaerobic (living without oxygen) amoeboid prokaryote ingested small aerobic (living with oxygen) bacteria and stabilized them instead of digesting them. This idea is known as the endosymbiont hypothesis. According to this hypothesis, the aerobic bacteria developed into mitochondria, which are the sites of aerobic respiration. Ingestion of prokaryotes that resembled present-day cyanobacteria could have led to the endosymbiotic development of chloroplasts in plants.

questions.text: Nuclear outer membrane is continuous with:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. The endoplasmic reticulum is often continuous with the plasma membrane and also appears to be in contact with the nuclear membrane when viewed through an electron microscope.
This option is incorrect. Golgi Appartus is a freely floating organelle found in the cell's cytoplasm.
This option is incorrect. Centrioles are located near the exterior surface of the nucleus but are not in contact with the nuclear membrane.
This option is incorrect. Lysosomes are small spherical digestive vesicles freely floating in the cell's cytoplasm. They are not continuous with the nuclear membrane.

questions.explanation: The endoplasmic reticulum is often continuous with the plasma membrane and also appears to be in contact with the nuclear membrane when viewed through an electron microscope.

questions.text: The exact replica of chromosome is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the correct option. A chromatid is one of the two identical halves of the chromosome that has been replicated in preparation for cell division.

This option is incorrect. Chromatin is a complex of DNA and proteins found in eukaryotic cells during the undividing stage. It is a network of very fine threads. Afterward, during cell division, this network condenses to form chromosomes.
This option is incorrect. Centromere is the place on the chromosome where spindle fibres are attached during cell division.
This option is incorrect. Plasmid is an additional DNA molecule present in some bacteria.

questions.explanation: A chromatid is one of the two identical halves of the chromosome that has been replicated in preparation for cell division.

questions.text: Nucleus is stained with:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. Nucleus is often deeply stained with basic dyes. This is because of presence of the chromatin material.
This option is incorrect. Fact: Nucleus are stained with basic dyes.
This option is incorrect. Fact: Nucleus are stained with basic dyes.
This option is incorrect. Fact: Nucleus are stained with basic dyes. Gram stains are used to test the nature of bacterial cell wall.

questions.explanation: Nucleus is often deeply stained with basic dyes. This is because of presence of the chromatin material. Haematoxylin stains basophilic substances. Hence, hematoxylin is commonly used to stain the nucleus.

questions.text: In muscle cells, which of these conduct impulses?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Nucleus contains the genetic material of the cell. It is not involved in the conduction of impulses.
This option is incorrect. Cytoplasm is the fluid present in the cell. It is 90% water. It does not conduct nerve impulses.

This option is incorrect. The functions of Golgi apparatus include cell secretions and modification of lipids and proteins into glycolipids and glycoproteins, respectively. It is not involved in nerve impulse conduction.

This is the correct answer. In muscle cells, smooth ER is called sarcoplasmic reticulum. It stores calcium ions that are essential for nerve impulse conduction. Hence, smooth ER is involved in the conduction of nerve impulses.

questions.explanation: In muscle cells, smooth ER is called sarcoplasmic reticulum. It stores calcium ions that are essential for nerve impulse conduction. Hence, smooth ER is involved in the conduction of nerve impulses.

questions.text: These are stacks of flattened membrane bounded sacs:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Lysosomes are small spherical digestive vesicles found in the cytoplasm of the cell.
This option is incorrect. Endoplasmic reticulum is not the stacks of sacs. Infact, it is a network of membrane-bounded channels that extends from nuclear membrane to the cell membrane.
This is the correct answer. The stack of flattened membrane-bounded sacs is called cisternae. Golgi apparatus consists of these stacks.
This option is incorrect. Nucleus is usually spherical, double membrane-bounded organelle that contains genetic material of the cell.

questions.explanation: The stack of flattened membrane-bounded sacs is called cisternae. Golgi apparatus consists of these stacks.

questions.text: In pancreas, granules containing enzymes are secreted by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the correct option. One of the most important functions of Golgi apparatus is cell secretions. Hence, Golgi apparatus is responsible for the secretion of granules containing enzymes by pancreas.
This option is incorrect. Endoplasmic reticulum is not involved in the secretion of any type of substance from the cell. It is the function of Golgi apparatus.
This option is incorrect. The only function of ribosomes is protein synthesis. They are not involved in cell secretions.
This option is incorrect. Lysosomes are digestive vesicles found in the cell. They are not involved in cell secretions.

questions.explanation: One of the most important functions of Golgi apparatus is cell secretions. Hence, Golgi apparatus is responsible for the secretion of granules containing enzymes by pancreas.

questions.text: It controls life and activities of cell:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. Nucleus contains all the genetic information of the cell. It is called the brain of cell. It controls all the life activites of the cell.

This option is incorrect. Mitochondria is the powerhouses of the cell. It generates energy for the cell. It does not control all the life activites of the cell. Cells can survive and divide without mitochondria.
This option is incorrect. Chloroplasts are resposible for photosynthesis and storage of food. They are not present in all types of cell. They do not control life activities of the cell.

This option is incorrect. ER is responsible for protein and lipid synthesis. It also provides support to the cell and causes detoxificatio of harmful chemicals. However, it does not control all the life activities of the cell.

questions.explanation: Nucleus contains all the genetic information of the cell. It is called the brain of cell. It controls all the life activites of the cell.

questions.text: The functional unit of Golgi apparatus is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Thylakoids are found in the chloroplast of the cell. They are not the functional unit of Golgi apparatus.
This option is incorrect. Oxysomes are also called F1 particles. They are found in the inner membrane of mitochondria. They are not the functional unit of Golgi apparatus.
This option is incorrect. Cristae are the infoldings present in the inner mitochondrial membrane. They are not the functional unit of Golgi apparatus.
This is the correct answer. Golgi apparatus consists of stacks of flattened membrane-bound sacs called cisternae. Cisternae is the functional unit of Golgi apparatus.

questions.explanation: Golgi apparatus consists of stacks of flattened membrane-bound sacs called cisternae. Cisternae is the functional unit of Golgi apparatus.

questions.text: How many of the following are membrane-bounded organelles? Mitochondria, centrioles, nucleolus, nucleus, chloroplast, ribosomes.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Only three of the above mentioned organelles are membrane-bounded. Nucleus, mitochondria, and chloroplast are membrane-bounded organelles. However, ribosomes, centrioles, and nucleolus are not bounded by any membrane.

This option is incorrect. Only three of the above-mentioned organelles are membrane-bounded. Nucleus, mitochondria, and chloroplast are membrane-bounded organelles. However, ribosomes, centrioles, and nucleolus are not bound by any membrane.

This option is the correct one. Only three of the above-mentioned organelles are membrane-bounded. Nucleus, mitochondria, and chloroplast are membrane-bounded organelles. However, ribosomes, centrioles, and nucleolus are not bound by any membrane.

This option is incorrect. Only three of the above-mentioned organelles are membrane-bounded. Nucleus, mitochondria, and chloroplast are membrane-bounded organelles. However, ribosomes, centrioles, and nucleolus are not bound by any membrane.

questions.explanation: Only three of the above-mentioned organelles are membrane-bounded. Nucleus, mitochondria, and chloroplast are membrane-bounded organelles. However, ribosomes, centrioles, and nucleolus are not bound by any membrane.

questions.text: The intake of liquid material by cell membrane by forming vacuole is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Phagocytosis is the intake of solid food particles by cell membrane.
This option is incorrect because endocytosis is a general term use for the intake of any kind of substance by the cell. However, the intake of liquid material is secifically called pinocytosis.
This is the correct answer. The intake of liquid material by the cell membrane in the form of vacuole is called pinocytosis.
This option is incorrect. Exocytosis is the removal of unwanted substances from the cell.

questions.explanation: The intake of liquid material by the cell membrane in the form of vacuole is called pinocytosis.

questions.text: Which one of the following is the site of oxidative phosphorylation in mitochondria?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. Cristae are the infoldings present in the inner mitochondrial membrane. They are the site for the electron transport chain(ETC). They contain F1 particles that are ATP synthase enzymes and generate ATP through oxidative phosphorylation.
This option is incorrect. Matrix is the site of Kreb's cycle. Oxidative phosphorylation does not occur here.
This option is incorrect. Outer mitochondrial membrane is smooth and freely permeable as it contains pores. It is not the site of Oxidative phosphorylation.
This option is incorrect. Ribosomes are the site for translation of messenger RNA. They are the protein factories of the cell. Oxidative phosphorylation does not occur here.

questions.explanation: Cristae are the infoldings present in the inner mitochondrial membrane. They are the site for the electron transport chain(ETC). They contain F1 particles that are ATP synthase enzymes and generate ATP through oxidative phosphorylation.

questions.text: Organelle involved in the synthesis of ATP is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Ribosomes are the protein factories of the cell. They are responsible for the formation of proteins by translation of messenger RNA.

Ths is the correct answer. Mitochondria are the powerhouses of the cell. They are responsible for ATP generation through cellular respiration. They contain ATP synsthase enzymes in the inner membrane at cristae. These enzymes catalyze the formation of ATP during electron transport chain(ETC).
This option is incorrect. Nucleus is the brain of cell. It contains all the genetic information of the cell in the form of DNA. It is not responsible for ATP formation.
This option is incorrect. Centrioles are the site of attachment of spindle fibres during cell division. So, they are important for cell division and are not involved in ATP synthesis.

questions.explanation: Mitochondria are the powerhouses of the cell. They are responsible for ATP generation through cellular respiration. They contain ATP synsthase enzymes in the inner membrane at cristae. These enzymes catalyze the formation of ATP during electron transport chain(ETC).

questions.text: Peptidoglycan or murein is special or distinctive feature of cell wall in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Algal cell wall is mainly composed of cellulose. It does not contain peptidoglycan.
This option is incorrect. Fungal cell wall is made up of chitin. It does not contain peptidoglycan.

This is the correct answer. Bacterial cell wall is made up of unique macromolecule called peptidoglycan or murein. It is the distinctive feature of prokaryotic cell. Bacteria is a prokaryotic organism.
This option is incorrect. Plant cell wall is mainly made up of cellulose. It does not contain peptidoglycan.

questions.explanation: Bacterial cell wall is made up of unique macromoclecule called peptidoglycan or murein. It is the distinctive feature of prokaryotic cell. Bacteria is a prokaryotic organism.

questions.text: In mitochondria, small knob-like structures called F1 particles are found in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The outer mitochondrial membrane is smooth and has pores that allow free movement of substances. It does not contain knob-like structures or F1 particles.
This option is incorrect. Outer compartment is the space between inner and outer mitochondrial membranes. It does not contain F1 particles.
This is the correct answer. The inner membrane of mitochondria has infoldings called cristae. Small knob-like structures are present on cristae. These knob-like structures are called F1 particles.

This option is incorrect. The inner compartment is the space enclosed by the inner mitochondrial membrane. It contains a fluid called matrix that contains many enzymes required for certain metabolic processes.

questions.explanation: The inner membrane of mitochondria has infoldings called cristae. Small knob-like structures are present on cristae. These knob-like structures are called F1 particles.

questions.text: Which component of the cell is concerned with cell secretions?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Plasma membrane is not concerned with cell secretions. The subtances that need to be secreted out of cell are processed and closed in vesicles by Golgi complex. These vesicles are then budded off from plasma membrane.
This is the correct answer. The main function of Golgi complex is cell secretion. The substances that need to be secreted out of the cell are processed and closed in vesicles by Golgi apparatus.

This option is incorrect. Cytoskeleton is involved in the formation of several organelles like cilia, flagella, centrioles etc. It is also responsible for movement of cyclosis and amoeboid movements. It is not concerned with cell secretions.
This option is incorrect. Micochondria are the site of cellular respiration and ATP synthesis. Cell secretion is not their function.

questions.explanation: The subtances that need to be secreted out of cell are processed and closed in vesicles by Golgi complex. These vesicles are then budded off from plasma membrane.

questions.text: The _ model of plasma membrane suggests that proteins are embedded in a lipid bilayer.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. According to this model, the lipid bilayer is sandwiched between inner and outer protein layers.
This is the correct answer. According to this model, The protein layers are not continuous and are not confined to the surface of the membrane but are embedded in lipid bilayer in a mosaic manner.
This option is incorrect. The studies on permeability of membrane explains that plasma membrane is selectively permeable. It means that it allows only selective substances to pass through it.
This option is incorrect. Ultracentrifugation is a technique used to separate cellular components by spinning the ultracentrifuge rotor at extremely high speed.

questions.explanation: According to fluid mosaic model, the protein layers are not continuous and are not confined to the surface of the membrane but are embedded in lipid bilayer in a mosaic manner.

questions.text: Lipid metabolism is the function of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Mitochondria is involved in cellular respiration and ATP synthesis. Lipid metabolism is not its function.

This option is incorrect. Smooth ER of the muscle cells is termed as sarcoplamsic reticulum. It stores calium ions that are needed for mucle contraction. Hence, it is involved in muscle contraction and not lipid metabolism.
This option is incorrect. RER has ribosomes attached to its surface. Ribosomes are protein factories. Hence, the RER os involved in protein synthesis and not lipid metabolism.
This is the correct answer. Fact: Smooth endoplasmic reticulum(SER) is involved in lipid metabolism.

questions.explanation: Fact: Smooth endoplasmic reticulum(SER) is involved in lipid metabolism.

questions.text: Plasma membrane is chemically composed of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. In addition to lipids, plasma membrane also contain proteins and traces of carbohydrates.

This is the best choice because plasma membrane is mainly composed of lipids and proteins. Chemically, it contains about 60-80% of lipids and 20-40% of proteins. However, it also contains traces of carbohydrates.
This option is incorrect because mainly plasma membrane is composed of lipids and proteins. It contains 60-80% lipids and 20-40% proteins.
This option is incorrect. Glycoproteins are present in very small queantities. The main structure of plasma membrane is chemically composed of 60-80% lipids and 20-40% proteins.

questions.explanation: The plasma membrane is mainly composed of lipids and proteins. Chemically, it contains about 60-80% of lipids and 20-40% of proteins. However, it also contains traces of carbohydrates.

questions.text: Endoplasmic reticulum consists of a system of flattened membrane bounded sacs which are named as:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Cristae are the infoldings present in the inner mitochondrial membrane.
This option is incorrect. Lamellae are the membranes of thylakoid sacs that are present in chloroplast.
This is the correct answer. ER consists of a network of membrane-bounded tubules or sacs are called cisternae.
This option is incorrect. ER consists of a network of membrane-bounded tubules or sacs that are specifically called cisternae.

questions.explanation: ER consists of a network of membrane-bounded tubules or sacs are called cisternae.

questions.text: When a glycoprotein is being synthesized for secretion from a cell, which route is it most likely to take?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Golgi apparatus→ Rough endoplasmic reticulum→ Smooth endoplasmic reticulum
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. For the secretion of glyoprotein from the cell, protein is first synthesized by the ribosomes on RER. This protein then travel through RER to SER to be transferred to the Golgi apparatus through transport vesicles. On reaching the Golgi apparatus, this protein is modified into glycoprotein and then budded off from Golgi apparatus as goldi vesicles. These vesicles are then secreted out of the cell.

options.text: Rough endoplasmic reticulum→ Golgi apparatus→ Smooth endoplasmic reticulum
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. For the secretion of glyoprotein from the cell, protein is first synthesized by the ribosomes on RER. This protein then travel through RER to SER to be transferred to the Golgi apparatus through transport vesicles. On reaching the Golgi apparatus, this protein is modified into glycoprotein and then budded off from Golgi apparatus as goldi vesicles. These vesicles are then secreted out of the cell.

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. For the secretion of glyoprotein from the cell, protein is first synthesized by the ribosomes on RER. This protein then travel through RER to SER to be transferred to the Golgi apparatus through transport vesicles. On reaching the Golgi apparatus, this protein is modified into glycoprotein and then budded off from Golgi apparatus as goldi vesicles. These vesicles are then secreted out of the cell.

options.text: Smooth endoplasmic reticulum→ Rough endoplasmic reticulum→ Golgi apparatus
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. For the secretion of glyoprotein from the cell, protein is first synthesized by the ribosomes on RER. This protein then travel through RER to SER to be transferred to the Golgi apparatus through transport vesicles. On reaching the Golgi apparatus, this protein is modified into glycoprotein and then budded off from Golgi apparatus as goldi vesicles. These vesicles are then secreted out of the cell.

questions.explanation: For the secretion of glyoprotein from the cell, protein is first synthesized by the ribosomes on RER. This protein then travel through RER to SER to be transferred to the Golgi apparatus through transport vesicles. On reaching the Golgi apparatus, this protein is modified into glycoprotein and then budded off from Golgi apparatus as goldi vesicles. These vesicles are then secreted out of the cell.

questions.text: Granular endoplasmic reticulum is mainly concerned with:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Proteolysis is the breakdown of proteins into smaller polypeptides or amino acids by proteolytic enzymes. It does not occur on granular ER. Granular ER is called so because it has ribosomes attached to its surface. These ribosomes are responsible for protein synthesis.

This is the correct answer. Granular ER is called so because it has ribosomes attached to its surface. These ribosomes are responsible for protein synthesis. Proteins contain polypeptide chains synthesized by peptide bond formation between amino acids.
This option is incorrect. A glycosidic bond is formed between carbohydrate molecules, resulting in the formation of polysaccharides. Polysaccharides are not synthesized on granular or rough ER.

This option is incorrect. Granular ER is called so because it has ribosomes attached to its surface. These ribosomes are responsible for protein synthesis. Fatty acids and cholesterol are not synthesized by it.

questions.explanation: Granular ER is called so because it has ribosomes attached to its surface. These ribosomes are responsible for protein synthesis. Proteins contain polypeptide chains synthesized by peptide bond formation between amino acids.

questions.text: Pinocytosis and phagocytosis are accomplished by the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The intake of solid particles or liquid materials in the form of vacuole is called phagocytosis and pinocytosis, respectively. Nuclear membrane is porous which allow free movement of substances accross it. It does not require to intake materials in the form of vacuole.

This option is incorrect. Outer mitochondrial membrane is porous and allow free movement of substances accross it. It does not require to take in materials in the form of vacuoles by phagocytosis or pinocytosis.

This is the correct answer. The cell membrane or plasma membrane is very selectively permeable. It allows only a few substances to pass through it through diffusion. However, it can intake solid or liquid food particles needed by the cell in the form of food vacuole by phagocytosis and pinocytosis, respectively.
This option is incorrect. Endoplasmic reticulum does not intake any substance by phagocytosis or pinocytosis.

questions.explanation: The cell membrane or plasma membrane is very selectively permeable. It allows only a few substances to pass through it through diffusion. However, it can intake solid or liquid food particles needed by the cell in the form of food vacuole by phagocytosis and pinocytosis, respectively.

questions.text: Which one of the following eukaryotic cell structures does not contain DNA?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Nucleus is the brain of the cell. It contains all the genetic information of the cell in the form of DNA.

This option is incorrect. Mitochondria are thought to have a symbiotic origin with bacteria. Detailed studies of mitochondria under an electron microscope show that a small circular DNA molecule is present in the mitochondrial matrix.
This is the correct answer. Endoplasmic reticulum is a network of membrane-bound tubules. It does not contain DNA.

This option is incorrect. Chloroplasts are also thought to have symbiotic origin with bacteria. Detailed studies of chloroplasts under an electron microscope show that a small circular DNA molecule is present in the stroma of chloroplast.

questions.explanation: The endoplasmic reticulum is a network of membrane-bound tubules. It does not contain DNA. The nucleus is the brain of the cell. It contains all the genetic information of the cell in the form of DNA. Mitochondria and chloroplasts are thought to have a symbiotic origin with bacteria. Detailed studies of mitochondria chloroplasts under an electron microscope show that a small circular DNA molecule is present in the mitochondrial matrix and stroma of chloroplast.

questions.text: Which one of the following would exocrine cells be expected to contain as a result of their function?

options.text: Increased amounts of rough endoplasmic reticulum
options.explanation: Exocrine cells have secretory function so an increased activity of such cells means increase in number of proteins or other secretions which are formed by ribosomes attached to RER.

The most important function of Golgi complex is the processing of cell secretions. Therefore these organelles are abundant in secretory cells. The cell secretions mainly consist of proteins. questions.text: The outermost layer in a typical plant cell would be_.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: If middle lamella is absent, primary cell wall is the outermost layer.
It is the innermost layer.
If middle lamella is present, it is the outermost layer of plant cell wall.
It is present on the inner surface of cell wall.

questions.explanation: The cell wall in plants and few algae consists of three important layers: 1) The primary cell wall is the outermost thin, flexible and extensible layer. 2) The secondary cell wall is a thick layer present inside the primary cell wall. 3) The middle lamella is a layer rich in pectins which form plasmodesmata connections between adjacent cell walls. This layer cements the cell walls of two adjacent plant cells. It is the first formed outermost layer (cell plate formed during cell division forms the middle lamella or lamellum). So, the correct answer is 'Middle lamella, if present'
